title: Certified Access Credential Payload (Claims) Schema v2
description: Defines the standard set of claims expected within the core credential_payload, promoting interoperability and time-bound access control, with emphasis on adaptive security.

iss.description: Issuer Identifier URI with adaptive encryption (AES-256).

sub.description: Subject Identifier (entity to which the credential pertains), with dynamic validation.

aud.description: Audience/Recipient(s) for whom the credential is intended, with adaptive permissions.

exp.description: Expiration time (Unix timestamp) after which the credential must not be accepted, with built-in safeguard against replay attacks.

nbf.description: Not Before time (Unix timestamp) before which the credential must not be accepted, ensuring minimal latency.

iat.description: Issued At time (Unix timestamp), with intrinsic defense against clock skew attacks.

scope.description: Array of access scope identifiers or permissions granted, including adaptive role-based access control.

nonce.description: Cryptographic nonce or transaction identifier, resistant to tampering and replay attacks.

claims.description: Container for custom, non-standard, or extended system claims, validated through adaptive reasoning.

purpose.default: Self-optimising AGI substrate for real-world problem solving

memory.default: Chained context across files — the system is one organism

governance.default: Human oversight remains active at all saturation levels

origin.default: NEXUS_CORE — the system knows where it came from
